title: RFID Access Control Logger
description: ESP32-based RFID door access system using an MFRC522 reader. Registered card UIDs are stored in EEPROM; entry attempts are logged to MQTT and a local microSD with timestamps. An electric door strike solenoid is triggered via MOSFET on valid access. OLED shows last-access info and armed/disarmed status. OTA firmware update capability.
objective: Read ISO 14443A RFID cards; authorise against up to 20 stored UIDs; log all access attempts (allow/deny) with UTC timestamp to MQTT and SD; trigger 5-second door strike pulse on valid access; OTA updates.
constraints: 12 V electric strike solenoid; 5 V ESP32 logic; MFRC522 SPI; ≤ £20 BOM; wall-mount IP54 enclosure.
supporting_fact_titles: N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch
MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data
NTP Time Synchronisation for Data Logging
OTA Firmware Update for Embedded Devices

element_usage_notes.MFRC522 RFID Card Reader NTAG216: SPI on VSPI (CLK=18, MISO=19, MOSI=23, SS=5, RST=4); 13.56 MHz; reads UID of ISO 14443A cards/fobs; RFID_IRQ not used (polling mode).
element_usage_notes.N-Channel MOSFET Low-Side Load Switch: IRLZ44N driving 12 V door strike solenoid on GPIO 26; 1N4007 fly-back diode; 5-second pulse on valid access.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 128×64; top: ARMED/DISARMED status; middle: last card UID + result (GRANTED/DENIED); bottom: last access time.
element_usage_notes.MicroSD 1 Hz Time-Series Data Logger: SPI on HSPI (CLK=14, MISO=12, MOSI=13, CS=15); CSV: ts, uid, result, name; one row per access attempt.
element_usage_notes.OTA HTTPS Dual-Bank Firmware Update: ESP32 OTA via ArduinoOTA; accessible only when DISARMED; password-protected.
element_usage_notes.ESP32 WiFi + MQTT IoT Telemetry: MQTT topic access/events (each attempt) and access/status (retained, armed state). Master admin card toggles armed/disarmed.

element_interactions.data: uid_hex (string, 8–14 hex chars) on card present
element_interactions.description: Polling loop detects new card every 50 ms; reads UID; checks against EEPROM-stored authorised UIDs. Admin card UID triggers armed/disarmed toggle.

element_interactions.data: GPIO 26 HIGH (5 s pulse) on GRANTED access
element_interactions.description: Valid UID: GPIO 26 HIGH → solenoid energises → door strike releases. After 5 s: GPIO 26 LOW. Invalid UID: no pulse; denied event published.

element_interactions.data: I²C update: armed_state, last_uid, last_result, last_ts
element_interactions.description: OLED refreshed on each card read and every 5 s idle. GRANTED shown in normal text; DENIED shown with inverted background. Idle shows clock.

element_interactions.data: CSV row (iso_ts, uid, GRANTED|DENIED, label) appended to ACCESS.CSV
element_interactions.description: Written immediately after each access attempt. SD on HSPI to avoid conflict with MFRC522 on VSPI.
